evidence.q1: Cross-reference resolves: 'Despite any contrary sentence, all recovery for Section 13 or Section 14 claims is limited to the amount stated in Section 16.' (Section 16).
evidence.q2: Term 'Security Incident' defined in Section 2 and used consistently in Section 6: 'after its incident lead confirms a Security Incident.'
evidence.q3: No vague time/quantity phrasing; e.g., Section 6 uses precise 'within thirty business days'.
evidence.q4: No archaic diction; modern phrasing like 'No amendment, modification, or waiver is effective unless in a writing signed by authorized representatives of both parties.' (Section 18).
evidence.q5: No internal contradiction or broken grammar; e.g., Section 5: 'Vendor will process Customer Data only to provide and support the services...' is clear and grammatical.
evidence.q6: Register consistent throughout; e.g., Section 2 and Section 17 both maintain same formal commercial legal style.
